g.title: n80
g.text: Transforms intake signals (food, water, substances) into physiological ingestion and absorption signals

g.title: n109
g.text: Transforms mechanical stimuli (loads, movement, posture) into signals for musculoskeletal and cardiovascular systems

g.title: n54
g.text: Transforms environmental gas signals into chemical levels and lung input for circulation

g.title: n88
g.text: Transforms environmental thermal signals into body heat load, cold/heat stress signals

g.title: n99
g.text: Mental stress perception of the human being

g.title: n121
g.text: Physical stress perception of the human being

g.title: n125
g.text: Observable state of the human being (e.g., temperature, blood pressure etc)

g.title: n48
g.text: Physiological load (e.g., thermal, mechanical, radiation etc)

g.title: n106
g.text: Internal physiological state (e.g., temperature, blood pressure etc)

g.title: n9
g.text: Homeostasis regulation system. Runs all the time and tries to keep important levels within ranges
